schema_note: v3 = 2026 model-year delta over the v2 dossiers (middrive-commuter.json + the Heybike/Engwe entries of ride1up-budget.json). Same row schema as v2: bikes[].stock_parts[] with optional 'years' (omit/null = all listed years) and optional 'year_notes'. Shared parts reuse v2 catalog_name strings EXACTLY so inventory merges. NEW in v3: per-bike 'update_type' — 'new_model' (full entry, 6-10 rows), 'year_variant_update' (material 2026 changes; full year-split treatment, prior-era rows reproduce v2 rows verbatim with only 'years' scoped), 'year_extension' (bike still sold in 2026 with NO component change; only rows whose 'years' string changed are re-listed, reproduced in full from v2 — 'carryover_note' says all other v2 rows apply unchanged), and 'discontinuation' (years capped; all v2 rows carry over unchanged). Bikes with no change at all (Como 3.0 already capped at 2025, old-platform Giant E+ 4 already 2020-2026, Heybike Mars 2.0 / Ranger S and Engwe Engine Pro with open-ended '-present' years all still sold) are deliberately ABSENT per mission rules. All comps this pass are 'estimated' (no 3+ confirmed eBay solds pulled); base = 50% of comp rounded down; null comp rows priced at or under 1000 cents.
bosch_2026_context: Bosch MY2026 (Eurobike 2025): new Performance Line CX 100Nm, new CX-R, new Performance Line PX (90Nm/700W), Kiox 400C top-tube display, PowerTube 540/600/750/800 Smart System packs. None of the five verified 2026 commuters in this cohort adopted the new MY26 motors — Trek/Gazelle/Cannondale carry their existing Smart System (or pre-smart, Cannondale) e-systems into 2026, so the existing shared Bosch catalog rows (Purion 200, Kiox 300, Smart System 4A charger, Compact PowerTube 400, PowerTube 625 Smart, PowerPack 545) keep merging unchanged. Watchlist for the next pass: Gazelle Avignon C380+ (new 2026 model, Bosch Performance Line PX 540-800Wh, single-source ebike24 mention — NOT entered without a second source) and any US arrival of Bosch PX/CX-gen5 commuters.

bikes.motor: Bosch Active Line 40Nm mid-drive (2019-2023, Gen 1-2); Bosch Active Line Plus 50Nm Smart System (2024+, Gen 3)

bikes.year_notes: 2026 VERIFIED STILL SOLD: Verve+ 2 Gen 3 and Verve+ 2 Lowstep Gen 3 are current on Bosch's US eBike pages and listed as '2026 Verve+ 2 Lowstep Gen 3 - 400Wh' at Trek retail. Same Gen 3 spec as 2024-2025: Active Line Plus 50Nm Smart System, internal Compact PowerTube 400 (optional PowerMore 250 extender), Purion 200, 2A compact charger. v2 years '2019-2025' extended to 2019-2026; Gen 3 rows below re-scoped 2024-2026. No component change found.
bikes.carryover_note: All other v2 rows (Bosch Purion display 2019-2023, Bosch PowerPack 400 battery 2019-2023, Bosch compact 2A charger, Bontrager Boulevard saddle, Bontrager H5 tire 2019-2023, Bontrager Satellite City pedals, Bontrager suspension seatpost) carry over from middrive-commuter.json v2 unchanged.

stock_parts.comp_note: Gen 3 (Smart System) only, per Trek's Verve+ generations page; 2026 model year verified on Bosch US / Trek retail listings. New Purion 200 selling at $171 with 29 sold on eBay (new units, not used takeoffs), so used takeoff estimated at $120. Smart System part; does not fit 2019-2023 bikes. Years extended 2024-2025 -> 2024-2026, row otherwise identical to v2.

upgrade.name: Bosch Kiox 300
upgrade.note: Verified $124-172 street. Smart System color display; drop-in for Purion 200 bikes.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Bosch Compact PowerTube 400 Smart System battery (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Gen 3 internal 400Wh pack per Trek's generations page; 2026 continuation verified ('2026 Verve+ 2 Lowstep Gen 3 - 400Wh' retail listing). Smart System part, not interchangeable with PowerPack-era packs. No used solds found; estimated in line with PowerPack 400. Lithium, ground shipping only. Range upgrades go through the Bosch PowerMore 250 extender. Years extended 2024-2025 -> 2024-2026, row otherwise identical to v2.

bikes.motor: Bosch Performance Line (Cruise) 75Nm Smart System mid-drive
bikes.battery: Bosch PowerPack 545 frame-integrated (545Wh standard; Trek lists 400/545/725/800Wh options by build)
bikes.year_notes: NEW ENTRY (bike existed since MY2024 but was absent from the catalog; it is in Trek's 2026 lineup alongside the Verve+ 2, so added this pass). Spec verified across Bosch US eBike pages (Verve+ 3 Gen 3, Verve+ 3 Lowstep Gen 3), Trek dealer listings and a 2026 '400Wh' retail listing: Bosch Performance Line Cruise 250W/75Nm Smart System, 545Wh Bosch battery fully encased in the frame (400/545/725/800Wh options exist — read the Wh sticker on intake), Purion 200 color control unit, Shimano CUES 9-speed, hydraulic discs, suspension seatpost, 27.5x2.4 puncture-resistant tires, ergonomic grips, lights, MIK rear rack + fenders. Charger spec NOT verified — no charger row without evidence. One step up from the Verve+ 2 Gen 3; shares the Smart System parts pool.

stock_parts.comp_note: Verified on Bosch US and Trek dealer 2024-2026 listings ('Purion 200 all-in-one color display, fully networked'). Same shared Smart System catalog row as the Verve+ 2 Gen 3 / Allant+ 7 Gen 2 / Medeo T9 2024+. New $171 with 29 sold on eBay (new units); used takeoff estimated $120.

upgrade.name: Bosch Kiox 300
upgrade.note: Verified $124-172 street. Smart System drop-in for Purion 200 bikes.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Bosch PowerPack 545 on-tube Smart System battery (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Trek/Bosch list a 545Wh Bosch battery 'fully encased in the frame' — the Bosch PowerPack 545 frame variant, the SAME physical Smart System pack as the on-tube unit on 2024+ Gazelle Medeo T9s, so the shared catalog row is reused; mounting hardware differs by bike, note it in the listing. Trek sells 400/545/725/800Wh builds — relabel to the matching Bosch pack row if intake shows a different Wh sticker. No used market data (new SKU); estimated between the PowerTube 500 and 625 floors. NOT interchangeable with pre-smart packs. Lithium, ground shipping only.

Shimano CUES 9-speed verified on 2024-2026 dealer listings. CUES is Shimano's current mid-line and Linkglide parts have real replacement demand, but used takeoffs are still a $20-30 commodity; no solds pulled. New row — also the future home for CUES takeoffs off Verve+ 2 Gen 3 and Allant+ 7 Gen 2 intakes.

Dealer spec: 27.5in wheels with 2.4in puncture-resistant tires; exact Bontrager model name not verified, so the row is named by verified size only. House-brand takeoff tires move slow and cheap; floor per tire.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 27.5x2.0
upgrade.note: Verified $63-74 street. The flat-proof commuter default.

stock_parts.comp_note: Suspension seatpost verified on 2024-2026 dealer listings; assumed the same Bontrager comfort-post family as the Verve+ 2 (shared row) — confirm branding on intake. Basic spring posts trade $15-25 used; estimated low.

upgrade.name: Cane Creek Thudbuster ST G4
upgrade.note: Parallel linkage instead of a pogo spring. Night and day on chewed-up pavement.

stock_parts.comp_note: MIK rear rack + fenders verified on 2024-2026 dealer listings; same shared row as the Allant+ 7. Real crash-replacement demand but no confirmed solds; estimated low.

New smart 625 runs ~$975; no used solds confirmed, estimated $400 floor. Smart System pack: NOT interchangeable with 2020-2022 PowerTubes. Lithium, ground shipping only. Years extended 2023-2025 -> 2023-2026, row otherwise identical to v2.

Gen 2 ships the Smart System-era 4A charger; continues on the 2026 bike. Priced in line with the pre-smart 4A sold band but no smart-specific solds isolated; estimated. Verify plug/generation compatibility before cross-selling to pre-2023 owners. Years extended 2023-2025 -> 2023-2026, row otherwise identical to v2.

bikes.motor: Specialized 2.0 (Brose-based) 70Nm mid-drive, both eras (2022-2025 gen and the 2026 redesign both list 250W/70Nm)
bikes.battery: Specialized U2-710 710Wh (2022-2025); 710Wh frame-integrated pack in the redesigned 2026 frame (cross-generation interchange NOT verified)
bikes.year_notes: MATERIAL 2026 CHANGE — the Vado got a full redesign for MY2026 (specialized.com Turbo Vado 4.0 product page; 99spokes 2026 Turbo Vado 4.0 / Step-Through / IGH entries): new less-angular frame, NEW MasterMind H3 display (Apple Find My, motor locking, over-the-air updates via the Specialized app), Shimano Deore XT 12-speed drivetrain, TRP hydraulic disc brakes, and a move to 700x47c tubeless-ready tires (from the 650b Pathfinder era). Motor stays a 250W/70Nm Specialized 2.0-family full-power unit; battery stays 710Wh, but whether the 2026 pack physically interchanges with the 2022-2025 U2-710 is NOT verified — treated as a separate row until proven. $4,000 MSRP. Prior-era rows below are v2 rows re-scoped 2022-2025 where the 2026 bike verifiably changed; rows where 2026 carryover is unverified (saddle, rack, grips, pedals) are also scoped 2022-2025 with no 2026 row invented. v2's warning still stands: pre-2022 Vado 4.0s are a third, older platform — grade separately.

stock_parts.comp_note: v2 row re-scoped: MasterMind TCD family is the 2022-2025 generation only — the 2026 redesign moved to the MasterMind H3. New TCD-w 2 retails ~$260; no confirmed used solds, estimated low at $120. Strong replacement demand since nothing else fits.

NEW 2026 row: MasterMind H3 verified on Specialized's 2026 Vado listings (Apple Find My integration, motor locking, OTA updates). New-generation part with zero used-market history; estimated above the TCD's $120 on newer-part scarcity. Does NOT fit 2022-2025 Vados.

v2 row re-scoped to the 2022-2025 generation. New sells $783 at LordGun. No used solds found; estimated low at $400 for a healthy pack. Lithium, ground shipping only, proprietary so demand is captive. Does NOT fit 2019-2021 Vados (604Wh platform); fit in the redesigned 2026 frame NOT verified.

NEW 2026 row: the redesigned Vado keeps a 710Wh full-power pack (Specialized 2026 listings), but the new frame means interchange with the 2022-2025 U2-710 is NOT verified — kept as a separate row until a physical cross-check or Specialized part number proves they merge. Priced at the U2-710 floor. Lithium, ground shipping only.

Proprietary Rosenberger-plug charger, unchanged 2022-2025; Specialized has kept one Turbo charger standard across the full-power range, so the row stays unsplit — but confirm the 2026 bike's plug on first intake before merging its charger here. Real lost-charger replacement demand. No confirmed solds found, estimated low at $60.

v2 row re-scoped: the reflective-wall 650b OEM tire belongs to the 2022-2025 generation — the 2026 redesign runs 700x47c tubeless-ready rubber. Sells but slowly; no solds found, low estimate per tire.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 27.5x2.0
upgrade.note: Verified $63-74 street. Heavier, slower, and you will never fix a flat in the rain again.

stock_parts.comp_note: NEW 2026 row: 700x47c tubeless-ready verified on Specialized's 2026 listings; exact tire model name not yet published consistently, so the row is named by verified size. Update the name once intake confirms the model. Low estimate per tire.

NEW 2026 row: Deore XT 12-speed verified on Specialized's 2026 Vado 4.0 listings — a genuinely valuable takeoff for once (XT mechs retail $110-130 and have real MTB demand). No solds pulled this pass, estimated $60 used; likely upgradeable to 'solid' quickly given XT liquidity.

NEW 2026 row: TRP hydraulics verified on Specialized's 2026 Vado 4.0 listings (exact caliper model not yet confirmed — note it on intake). TRP is a real brand with aftermarket demand; complete takeoff set estimated $80, no solds pulled.
v2 row re-scoped 2022-2025; the 2026 redesign's saddle spec is unverified, so no 2026 row is created. Specialized saddles carry brand value used but no solds confirmed. Estimated low.

upgrade.name: Brooks C17 Cambium
upgrade.note: Rubber Brooks, zero break-in, shrugs off rain. Right saddle for an all-weather Vado.

stock_parts.comp_note: v2 row re-scoped 2022-2025; 2026 rack spec unverified on the redesigned frame, no 2026 row created. Model-specific rack with replacement demand from crash repairs. No solds found, estimated low.
v2 row re-scoped 2022-2025; 2026 grip spec unverified. Used grips are near worthless unless mint. Floor pricing.

upgrade.name: Ergon GP1 Evo grips
upgrade.note: Wing-shaped support that kills hand numbness on long commutes. Cheapest comfort upgrade there is.

stock_parts.comp_note: v2 row re-scoped 2022-2025; 2026 pedal spec unverified. No meaningful resale market for stock platforms.

upgrade.name: Shimano PD-EH500
upgrade.note: Flat plus SPD in one pedal. Standard first upgrade on a 28 mph commuter.

bikes.year_notes: NEW ENTRY: the 2026 Turbo Como line starts at the 4.0 (99spokes 2026 shows Como 4.0 / 4.0 IGH / 5.0 IGH; no 2026 Como 3.0 found — the 3.0's v2 years of 2022-2025 stand and it is deliberately absent from v3). The Como 4.0 is the current volume Como: same 2022 MasterMind platform as the Vado — Specialized 2.0 motor (250W/70Nm), U2-710 710Wh, MasterMind TCD display, Turbo System Lock, 650b Nimbus 2 tires, 80mm suspension fork, suspended seatpost, DRYTECH fenders, 27kg HD rear rack, lights (Specialized 2026 product pages, EBR Como 4.0 review, dealer 2026 listings). Verified same-generation through 2026 — the H3-display redesign hit the 2026 Vado, NOT the Como; check 2026 Como intakes for a TCD anyway and flag if an H3 shows up. Shares e-system rows with the Vado 4.0 (2022-2025 era) and battery/charger with the whole 2022+ full-power family.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same 2022+ MasterMind TCD family row shared with the Vado 4.0 (2022-2025) and Como 3.0. New ~$260, no confirmed used solds, estimated low at $120.

710Wh verified on Specialized 2026 Como 4.0 listings — the same U2-710 shared row as the 2022-2025 Vado 4.0. New sells $783 at LordGun; no used solds found, estimated $400 floor. Lithium, ground shipping only, captive demand.
Shared proprietary Rosenberger-plug charger across the 2022+ full-power Turbo family. No confirmed solds, estimated low at $60.
Shared row with the Como 3.0 — same wide comfort saddle family. Sells used but slow; no solds confirmed, low estimate.

upgrade.name: Brooks B67
upgrade.note: Sprung leather made for upright bikes. Exactly what a Como rider actually wants.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared row with the Como 3.0 — OEM 650b reflective tire, consistent across the generation and trims. No solds found, estimated low per tire.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 27.5x2.0
upgrade.note: Flat protection worth every gram on a bike this heavy.

stock_parts.comp_note: 27kg-rated HD rack verified on 2026 listings; shared row with the Como 3.0. Integrated rack with crash-replacement demand. No solds found, estimated low.
Same shared part as the Vado/Como 3.0 entries. Floor pricing.

upgrade.name: Ergon GP1 Evo grips
upgrade.note: Palm support for upright riding position. Cheap fix for numb hands.

stock_parts.comp_note: 80mm suspension fork verified on 2026 Como 4.0 listings (the 4.0's step up over the rigid-ish 3.0); exact make not confirmed — note it on intake. Thin used market, estimated $50, no solds.

bikes.motor: Bosch Smart System Performance Line Speed 85Nm mid-drive (Class 3)
bikes.year_notes: NEW ENTRY: Gazelle's flagship US commuter above the Ultimate, launched 2023 and current on gazellebikes.com for 2026. Spec verified on Gazelle's US product page: Bosch Smart System Performance Line Speed 85Nm, 750Wh battery (Gazelle marketing: '85Nm motor and 750Wh battery'), Bosch Kiox 300 removable color display, Bosch standard 4A charger, Gates CDX belt + Enviolo C380 stepless hub (near-zero drivetrain takeoffs, same as the Ultimate — no rows), SR Suntour Mobie45 75mm fork with lockout, Schwalbe Marathon Efficiency tires (60-584), Ryde ZAC 25 rims, Selle Royal Essenza Plus gel saddle, Ergon GP1-L grips, aluminum MIK HD carrier, Herrmans/Spanninga lights. Every Bosch/Selle Royal/Ergon/Suntour row below reuses the existing shared catalog string so inventory pools with the Ultimate C380 and Medeo.

Biggest pack in the commuter cohort; new retail runs ~$1,100+, no used solds found, estimated $450 floor just above the PowerTube 625's $400. Smart System only; NOT interchangeable with pre-smart PowerTubes. Lithium, ground shipping only.

Gazelle US spec: 'Bosch Standard Charger, 4A' on this Smart System bike — merged into the existing Smart System 4A charger row. Priced in line with the pre-smart 4A sold band; no smart-specific solds isolated.

upgrade.name: Brooks B17
upgrade.note: The Dutch bike deserves the English saddle. Lasts decades.

stock_parts.comp_note: Verified GP1-L on Gazelle's US Eclipse page; merges into the existing Ergon grips row (C380+ trim). Genuine Ergon so it prices above generic grips; no solds confirmed.

MIK HD carrier verified on Gazelle's US Eclipse page; merged into the existing Gazelle rack row — note MIK HD compatibility when listing. Crash-replacement demand, thin US supply. No solds found, estimated low.

bikes.motor: Giant SyncDrive Sport 2 75Nm mid-drive (new Giant E+ system, Smart Gateway 2.0 era)
bikes.year_notes: NEW ENTRY: Giant replaced the whole Explore E+ platform for MY2026 (Giant 2026 product pages; Cycling Electric first ride of the E+ 0; ebike24 2026-novelties roundup). New line runs E+ 0 / E+ 1 (800Wh, SyncDrive Pro 2 85Nm) down to E+ 2 / E+ 3 (625Wh) — there is NO E+ 4 tier in the 2026 line, which closes out the old giant-explore-e-plus-4 listing at its existing 2020-2026 run (old stock; entry unchanged, deliberately absent from v3). The E+ 2 is the volume trim, verified on Giant UK/dealer 2026 listings: SyncDrive Sport 2 75Nm with 400% support, EnergyPak 625Wh, RideDash Evo 2 color display + RideControl Ergo 4 remote + Go 2 button, EnergyPak 4A smart charger (60% in 2.5h), SR Suntour NEX 63mm fork, Shimano Alivio M3100 9-speed, Giant Crosscut Gravel 2 700x45 tubeless-ready tires, integrated 27kg rack. CAUTION: 2026 availability confirmed via UK/EU retail; US-market trim mix not yet confirmed — most US takeoff flow will stay 2020-2022 old-platform bikes near-term. NONE of the new-platform e-parts interchange with old-platform (Yamaha-based SyncDrive / EnergyPak Smart 500) bikes.

stock_parts.stock_part: Giant RideDash Evo 2 display with RideControl Ergo 4 remote
stock_parts.comp_note: NEW row: RideDash Evo 2 color LCD + Ergo 4 dual-sided remote verified on 2026 dealer listings. New-platform part, zero used history; estimated above the old Dash 2's $50 on recency. Does NOT fit pre-2026 Explore E+ bikes.

NEW row: 625Wh EnergyPak verified on 2026 E+ 2 listings (Giant claims up to 2,300 charge cycles on the new packs). No used market yet; estimated just above the old Smart 500's $300 floor, discounted for Giant's smaller buyer pool vs Bosch. NOT interchangeable with old-platform EnergyPak Smart packs. Lithium, ground shipping only.

2026 listings name the EnergyPak 4A smart charger (60% in 2:30) — same name as the existing shared row, so it merges; VERIFY the plug matches old-platform chargers before cross-selling across the 2026 platform break. Proprietary plug, captive demand. No solds found, estimated $50.
Alivio M3100 verified on 2026 E+ 2 dealer listings. Low-mid Shimano; used mechs are a $15-25 commodity. No solds pulled.
NEW row: Suntour NEX 63mm verified on 2026 E+ 2 listings. Entry steel-sprung commuter fork, weak aftermarket; $30 repair-part estimate, no solds.

Crosscut Gravel 2 700x45 verified on 2026 E+ 2 dealer listings — same tire (and same shared catalog row) as the 2020-2022 old-platform E+ 4. House-brand tire, weak used demand; floor estimate per tire.

upgrade.name: Schwalbe Marathon E-Plus 700c
upgrade.note: Same flat-proof casing in 700c fitment. Check clearance against the stock 45mm width.

stock_parts.comp_note: 2026 platform ships integrated racks rated to 27kg (Cycling Electric / ebike24 coverage). Merged into the existing Giant rack row; note the 27kg MIK-style rating when listing. No solds found, estimated low.

bikes.year_notes: 2026 VERIFIED STILL SOLD, UNCHANGED: Adventure Neo 4 remains current on cannondale.com and Bosch's ePowered pages with the identical pre-smart spec every year of its run — Bosch Active Line 40Nm, PowerTube 400, Purion display, 2A compact charger (Bosch page confirms 'Active Line Motor and 400Wh battery'). v2 years '2021-2024' extended to 2021-2026. Remarkably, this keeps a PRE-SMART Bosch system in production in 2026 — the cheapest current source of Purion/PowerTube 400 takeoffs. The v2 note about Altus vs microSHIFT drivetrain swaps by production run still applies. No stock_parts rows re-listed because every v2 row is year-unscoped (years null) and unchanged.
bikes.carryover_note: ALL v2 rows (Bosch Purion display, Bosch PowerTube 400 battery, Bosch compact 2A charger, Cannondale Fitness Ergo saddle, Kenda Kwick Seven.5 tire, Cannondale ergo grips, Wellgo platform pedals) carry over from middrive-commuter.json v2 unchanged.

bikes.motor: Mivice 500W mid-drive, 105Nm (1000W peak), torque sensor — Heybike's 'Galaxy eDrive System' family
bikes.blurb: The budget mid-drive outlier: a $1,000-1,400 street price fat-tire commuter with a real Mivice mid-motor and torque sensor — the only bike in the budget cohort whose motor architecture matches the premium half of this dossier.
bikes.year_notes: NEW ENTRY: launched at CES 2025, current and heavily promoted through 2026 (heybike.com product page; Heybike's own 'ALPHA vs Ranger S vs Mars 2.0' comparison; repeated 2026 sale coverage on Electrek/9to5Toys at $999-1,398). Verified spec: Mivice 500W mid-drive at 105Nm with torque sensor, 680Wh removable UL 2271 pack, 28 mph, Shimano Altus 8-speed, hydraulic disc brakes (caliper brand unverified), 26x4.0 puncture-protected fat tires, hydraulic suspension fork, LCD display, rear rack + fenders stock, 400 lb payload, 6061 alloy frame, up to 60 mi range. Brake/tire/fork BRANDS are unverified budget OEM — noted per row.

stock_parts.comp_note: Model-specific removable pack, UL 2271 certified (a genuine resale plus in the budget cohort). Heybike sells spares direct, capping used value. No solds found; $150 estimate — tops the Heybike battery pile on capacity + certification.

upgrade.name: Alpha Spare Battery (OEM)
upgrade.note: Model-specific fit, UL-certified. OEM or nothing on a frame-fitted pack.

stock_parts.comp_note: Merges into the existing Heybike display row — only buyers are Heybike owners with cracked screens; new spares available cheap. $20 estimate.
NEW shared budget row (also fits Mars 3.0 / Ranger 3.0 Pro takeoffs): Altus 8-speed verified on the Alpha product page. Used Altus mechs are a $12-18 commodity; no solds pulled.

NEW row: 26x4 (not the 20x4 folder size — different pool). Brand unverified budget OEM. 26x4 fat takeoffs move slightly better than 20x4s to the Ranger/RadRover crowd; $25 the pair, estimated, no solds.

upgrade.name: Vee Tire Co Zigzag 26x4.0 (pair)
upgrade.note: Street-biased tread that quiets the hum and stretches the 680Wh pack.

stock_parts.comp_note: NEW row: hydraulic discs verified on the product page, caliper brand not stated (Heybike has used RSX and other no-names). No-name budget hydros have effectively no resale market. Parts-bin value.
NEW row (26in fat fork — not the folder-specific pool): unbranded hydraulic coil unit, no aftermarket demand found. Repair-part value only.
Merges into the existing generic budget saddle row (the row name says 'folder' but it is the same no-name saddle pool). No resale market.

upgrade.name: Cloud-9 Cruiser Select
upgrade.note: Wide, cheap, and it ends the saddle complaints on day one.

stock_parts.comp_note: NEW row: rack ships stock on the Alpha (and other Heybikes) — crash-replacement demand only, from other Heybike owners. $20 estimate, no solds.

bikes.motor: 750W Galaxy eDrive rear hub, 95Nm (1400W peak), torque sensor
bikes.blurb: The Mars 2.0's full-suspension successor: torque sensor, 95Nm, in-tube 624Wh pack and a 10-second fold at $1,199-1,299 — sold ALONGSIDE the Mars 2.0, which continues as the cheaper trim.
bikes.year_notes: NEW ENTRY: launched mid-2025 (Electrek review Aug 2025: '30 MPH full-suspension e-bike for just $1,199'; EBR, Electric Bike Report, Gear Diary reviews Sept-Oct 2025), headlined Heybike's CES 2026 X-series presence. Verified spec: 750W/1400W-peak Galaxy eDrive hub with 95Nm and torque sensor, 48V 13Ah 624Wh removable in-tube pack, Shimano Altus 8-speed, full suspension (front fork + rear shock; rear shock type unverified — likely coil, flagged), hydraulic disc brakes, 20x4 fat tires (brand unverified), 440 lb payload, ~$1,299. IMPORTANT: the Mars 2.0 (existing entry, '2023-present') REMAINS ON SALE as the value trim — its entry is unchanged and deliberately absent from v3; do not merge Mars 2.0 and 3.0 batteries (external vs in-tube packs).

stock_parts.comp_note: Model-specific in-tube pack — does NOT interchange with the Mars 2.0's external 600Wh pack. Heybike sells spares direct. Used budget packs move slow; $110 estimate, no solds found.

upgrade.name: Mars 3.0 Spare Battery (OEM)
upgrade.note: In-tube frame-specific pack. Mars 2.0 batteries will not fit.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shimano 8-speed confirmed across Mars 3.0 reviews; shared budget Altus row with the Alpha and Ranger 3.0 Pro. $12-18 used commodity.
Mapped to the shared 20x4 budget pool per v2 precedent (Heybike has shipped ChaoYang on this size); verify the sidewall on intake and relabel if Kenda. Supply swamps demand; $20 the pair.

upgrade.name: Vee Tire Co Speedster 20x4.0 (pair)
upgrade.note: Street slicks that drop the knobby drone and add real miles of range.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Heybike display row; repair-part demand only. $20 estimate.
Folder-specific no-name fork; merges into the existing budget-folder fork row per v2 precedent. No aftermarket demand.
NEW row: rear suspension verified in every review; shock type/brand not stated (likely coil). Folder-specific mount, no market found. Do not merge with the Ranger 3.0 Pro's adjustable AIR shock row.
Shared no-name saddle pool. No resale market.

upgrade.name: Cloud-9 Cruiser Select
upgrade.note: Same story as every budget folder: stock saddle lasts one long weekend.

stock_parts.comp_note: Hydraulic discs confirmed in reviews; brand not stated. Shared no-name Heybike hydro row. Parts-bin value.

bikes.blurb: Heybike's flagship folder: 720Wh, 90-mile claim, and the one genuinely interesting takeoff in the budget cohort — a rider-tunable air rear shock rated to 174 PSI.
bikes.year_notes: NEW ENTRY: reviews landed Sept-Oct 2025 (EBR, Electric Bike Report, Ebike Escape, The Gadgeteer) and it anchors Heybike's 2026 X-series marketing. Verified spec: 750W hub (1200W peak, 80Nm, torque sensor, 28 mph), 720Wh removable pack with 54.6V 2A charger, 65mm-travel hydraulic front fork, ADJUSTABLE AIR rear shock (174 PSI max), Kenda 20x4.0 puncture-resistant tires, Shimano Altus 8-speed, hydraulic disc brakes, 440 lb payload, $1,499 MSRP. Reviewer criticisms (noisy motor, cheap grips/pedals) confirm the usual budget-takeoff story outside the battery and shock. The Ranger S (existing entry, '2023-present') REMAINS ON SALE — unchanged, absent from v3; note Heybike now markets it as 'Ranger S 1000W', so verify motor label on Ranger S intakes.

stock_parts.comp_note: Biggest Heybike folder pack; model-specific fit (does NOT interchange with Ranger S or Mars packs). Heybike sells spares direct, capping used value. $130 estimate, no solds found.

upgrade.name: Ranger 3.0 Pro Spare Battery (OEM)
upgrade.note: Frame-specific pack. Ranger S batteries will not fit.

stock_parts.stock_part: Adjustable air rear shock (174 PSI max)
stock_parts.catalog_name: Heybike Air Rear Shock (Ranger 3.0 Pro takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: NEW row: the one budget-cohort suspension part with a pulse — a tunable air shock beats every no-name coil for resale, and other Ranger 3.0 Pro owners will pay for a replacement. Folder-specific mount limits the buyer pool. $40 estimate, no solds. Kept separate from the Mars 3.0's unverified (likely coil) shock row.
NEW row: Kenda-branded (verified in reviews), so it prices a notch above the ChaoYang/generic 20x4 glut — but only a notch. $25 the pair, estimated, no solds.

upgrade.name: Vee Tire Co Speedster 20x4.0 (pair)
upgrade.note: The stock tread is already street-ish; the Speedster does it quieter with more range.

stock_parts.comp_note: Altus 8-speed verified in reviews; shared budget Altus row with the Alpha and Mars 3.0. $12-18 used commodity.
Shared Heybike display row; repair-part demand only. $20 estimate.
Folder-specific no-name unit; merges into the existing budget-folder fork row per v2 precedent (same treatment as the Ranger S 'hydraulic' fork). No market found.
Hydraulic discs verified in reviews; brand not stated. Shared no-name Heybike hydro row. Parts-bin value.
Shared no-name saddle pool. No resale market.

upgrade.name: Cloud-9 Cruiser Select
upgrade.note: Step-thru cruiser posture wants a wide saddle. This is the one.

bikes.year_notes: YEARS CAPPED: Engwe's global store lists the EP-2 Pro as no longer available and points buyers to the upgraded EP-2 Boost (engwe.com, verified 2026-07). us.engwe.com still shows residual EP-2 Pro stock/gift-pack listings, so 2025 is kept as the final model year rather than 2024. v2 years '2021-present' -> '2021-2025'. No component changes to any row — the huge installed base keeps every v2 takeoff row alive for years of trade-in flow. Successor gets its own entry (engwe-ep-2-boost).
bikes.carryover_note: ALL v2 rows (Engwe EP-2 Pro 48V 13Ah Battery, Generic Mechanical Disc Brake Set, ChaoYang 20x4.0 Fat Tire Pair, Generic Comfort Saddle, Shimano Tourney 7-Speed Rear Derailleur, Engwe Stock Color LCD Display, Budget Folder Coil Fork) carry over from ride1up-budget.json v2 unchanged.

bikes.motor: 750W geared rear hub, 55Nm, with Boost button
bikes.blurb: The EP-2 Pro's official replacement: same 48V 13Ah formula with a Boost button, bumped 55Nm torque and 180mm rotors — and the same near-worthless no-name takeoffs, so the battery is still the only row that matters.
bikes.year_notes: NEW ENTRY: Engwe's global store now sells the EP-2 Boost and explicitly recommends it as the EP-2 Pro's replacement (engwe.com product page; US-market availability confirmed via retailer listings). Verified spec: 750W hub, 55Nm, 48V 13Ah pack, ~120km claimed assist range, upgraded 180mm disc brakes (hydraulic NOT confirmed — treated as mechanical per EP-2 line precedent until an intake proves otherwise), Shimano 7-speed, 20x4.0 fat tires, folding frame, 150kg max load. Rows reuse the v2 EP-2 Pro catalog strings wherever the part carried over; the battery gets its own row until pack-case interchange with the EP-2 Pro is physically verified.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same 48V 13Ah spec as the EP-2 Pro pack and possibly the same case — kept as a separate row until interchange is verified on an intake; merge the rows if Engwe's spare listing covers both. Smaller no-name-cell pack, wary buyers; $90 estimate, no solds.

upgrade.name: EP-2 Boost Spare Battery (OEM)
upgrade.note: Engwe sells spares direct. Match the model — do not assume EP-2 Pro packs swap in until verified.

stock_parts.stock_part: 180mm disc brake set (mechanical per line precedent; verify on intake)
stock_parts.comp_note: Engwe markets 'upgraded 180mm disc brakes' without claiming hydraulic; the EP-2 line has been mechanical throughout, so this merges into the v2 mechanical row with a bigger-rotor note. If an intake shows hydraulics, split the row then. Takeoff mechanical calipers are scrap-bin value.

upgrade.name: Tektro HD-E350 hydraulic brake kit
upgrade.note: A 70+ lb bike on cable discs is a dare. Best money you can put into an EP-2.

stock_parts.comp_note: Same shared 20x4 budget pool as the EP-2 Pro. Supply swamps demand; $20 the pair.

upgrade.name: Vee Tire Co Speedster 20x4.0 (pair)
upgrade.note: Kills the knobby hum and buys back a few miles of range.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shimano 7-speed verified on the product page; Tourney-class per line precedent. Near-free on the used market.

upgrade.name: Shimano Altus M310
upgrade.note: The standard Tourney escape hatch. Cheap and instant.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared Engwe display row; repair-part demand only from other Engwe owners. $20 estimate.
Shared no-name saddle pool. No resale market.

upgrade.name: Cloud-9 Cruiser Select
upgrade.note: Forty dollars against a saddle the bike maker spent four on.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared budget-folder fork row. No aftermarket demand found.
